text: Ever wonder why some animals flaunt their flaws? It's called the handicap principle. This idea suggests that costly traits signal strength. Imagine a peacock. Its extravagant feathers make it more visible to predators. But that risk attracts mates, proving fitness. It’s a risky show of confidence. So, the more extravagant the display, the stronger the individual. But it’s not without debate. Want to dive deeper into science? Like and follow for more fascinating insights!
